priority_basis: The canonical queue has no active frontier_research item. The latest owner-scoped handoff, R-028, required a real-browser runner for ordinary IndexedDB lifecycle scenarios while preserving explicit capability boundaries.
action: Implement a Playwright runner for two-tab contention and versionchange/forced-close across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit, with fail-closed capability gating for quota pressure and abrupt termination.
finding: Ordinary same-origin multi-page contention and versionchange closure can be represented in Playwright. Graceful context closure must not be substituted for abrupt process termination, and a non-persistent context does not prove on-disk persistence.
design_implication: Emit validated browser/scenario evidence for automatable cases and explicit blocked records for capability-gated cases. Keep transaction completion, replay observation, interruption injection, reopen observation, and persistence claims separate.
verification: A precommit prototype passed 5 deterministic Node tests with 0 failures. Committed-source execution and real-browser execution were not observed, so no browser outcome is claimed.
source_status: current official primary sources inspected on 2026-07-12
evidence_strength: official-source design plus committed implementation and deterministic test specification
uncertainty: The committed runner still requires repository-pinned Playwright browser binaries and a served same-origin fixture for execution evidence.
falsification_route: Run the committed test and browser matrix in CI; reject or revise the design if records fail validation, if browser event timelines do not distinguish the scenarios, or if a capability-gated scenario can be represented without the declared injector and reopen observer.
handoff: Add a minimal same-origin IndexedDB fixture and a CI job pinned to the repository's Playwright version. Execute Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit for two-tab contention and versionchange/forced-close, upload the six validated evidence records, and leave abrupt termination and quota pressure blocked unless platform-specific injection is available.
